title: heracleum alpinum subsp. palmatum
intro: Heracleum alpinum subsp. palmatum, also known as the alpine hog's fennel, is a subspecies of the Heracleum alpinum plant, belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and native to Europe and Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful for treating respiratory issues, wounds, and digestive problems. The health benefits of alpine hog's fennel include its ability to reduce inflammation, fight off infections, and ease symptoms of colds and flu. Alpine hog's fennel is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used in herbal remedies for its medicinal properties.
